extra.reason: Added application-level Keepalive (5s ping, 15s watchdog) to RFCOMM + TCP transports on both sides; closes the socket on silent BT drops so the existing reconnect path runs. On Glass, transport disconnect now triggers a 10s-grace screen-wake alert with large 'DISCONNECTED' text on the LiveCard, cleared on reconnect. 6 new Keepalive unit tests + existing TcpTransport roundtrip pass.

Switched mapsforge render theme from OSMARENDER to BIKER and added an XmlRenderThemeMenuCallback whitelisting only nature/road_detail/off_road. Hides POIs, house numbers, contour lines, transit/parking markers, and other categorized rules; roads/water/terrain (uncategorized) still render. Centralized in new MapTheme.kt, applied to both SnippetRenderer (Glass HUD) and RoutePreviewFragment (phone). Contrast improves as a side effect of removing visual noise — explicit contrast tuning deferred (would require either userScaleFactor bump or post-render ColorMatrix; both worth considering after a real-bike legibility test).
Closed
Closed

Stop button now keeps RideService + BT transport alive; selecting a new route reuses the existing connection via RideService.startRoute() instead of paying for a Bluetooth reconnect. Also tracks currentRouteId so stopRide() can send a matching RouteEnd(CANCELLED) to Glass.
Increased road/path contrast and size; replaced position dot with directional arrow.

Implemented: (1) initial-direction TTS on route start via PacketDispatcher.maybeSpeakInitialDirection; (2) remaining distance + ETA computed in RideService and sent as new Packet.Progress fields; (3) DisplayPrefs + 4-slot DisplaySettingsDialogFragment with gear icon in fragment_ride_controls.xml; (4) phone renders per phoneTop/phoneBottom prefs; (5) Packet.DisplayConfig (0x05) pushed to Glass on connect and on settings change.

Replaced brittle 2-consecutive off-route counter with a 7-of-last-10-fixes rolling window (RouteMatcher.kt) so GPS jitter / parallel-road flicker no longer resets detection. Reduced REROUTE_COOLDOWN_MS from 30s to 5s (RideService.kt) so a BRouter failure can't blow past the 30s budget. Added tests for trigger, flicker tolerance, and clearing.

MapView was added to map_container FrameLayout without an index, so it appended last and drew on top of orientation_toggle and display_settings overlay buttons. Fixed by inserting MapView at index 0 in RideControlsFragment.mountMap.
Multi-provider RealGpsSource (FUSED/GPS/NETWORK/PASSIVE) prevents stalls when raw GPS drops
On-device verification deferred per user; foundation code (osmand-api module, OsmAndAidlClient facade, OsmAndAidlDebugActivity, manifest queries) builds cleanly and is treated as validated.

TurnTypeMapping.kt + unit test added under phone-app/.../osmand. Built and tested clean. Fidelity gaps documented in class kdoc: (1) roundabout exit number is dropped by OsmAnd V2 AIDL (getValue() strips exitOut) — folded to KL/KR; (2) ADirectionInfo.isLeftSide is never set by V2 AIDL impl — treat as meaningless; (3) C (continue) and OFFR map to null so caller can skip/handle as deviation; (4) TRU collapses into TU. Recommendation: future protocol bump should add STRAIGHT + ROUNDABOUT to TurnKind for full fidelity, but the current 11-entry enum is workable for the spike.

ADirectionInfo cadence insufficient (0.34Hz, 7.5s max gap) and missing speed/remaining/eta — Option A still viable but requires registerForUpdates alongside. registerForUpdates validation folded into glass-nav-u1y. Full analysis in notes.

Added assets/spike-route.gpx (Brandenburg→Reichstag, 10 trkpts), OsmAndAidlClient.setLocation(), GpxRouteDriver (parses GPX, interpolates at tickHz, pushes via setLocation with timeToNotUseOtherGps=5s). SpikeSessionActivity auto-starts the driver on 'Start capture'. Updated SPIKE_TEST_INSTRUCTIONS.md and in-app README to drop the unreliable manual 'Simulate your position' step. Compile-clean.

Implemented OsmAndAidlClient.navigateGpx(snap=off) and wired into SpikeSessionActivity.startCapture. Same GPX serves as route source (navigateGpx) and position source (GpxRouteDriver.setLocation). Compile-clean. Awaiting next on-device capture to verify off-route warnings are gone and to assess voice-instruction quality without snap-to-road.

Added FileProvider (authority dev.glass.phone.spike.fileprovider) with res/xml/spike_file_paths.xml mapping the external-files spikes/ dir. SpikeSnippetCapture now wraps the tmp GPX in a FileProvider Uri, grantUriPermission(READ) to the bound OsmAnd package before getBitmapForGpx, revoke after. Switched to the Uri overload of CreateGpxBitmapParams. Status string also updated to suggest checking OsmAnd was opened once since install. assembleDebug clean.
See notes for full analysis. 0ha: ship-with-AIDL + overlay tweaks; getBitmapForGpx viable, no Option C needed. 51h: Option A — pipe OsmAnd's params.played array straight to Glass TTS.
See notes for full analysis. 0ha: ship-with-AIDL + overlay tweaks; getBitmapForGpx viable, no Option C needed. 51h: Option A — pipe OsmAnd's params.played array straight to Glass TTS.

Answered: no public OsmAnd AIDL exposes computed route polyline or turn list. ASelectedGpxFile is metadata-only; ADirectionInfo carries only next-turn distance/type/leftSide; navigate()/calculateRoute() return boolean. Three paths evaluated in spikes/20260525-205327/bk3-summary.txt — Path 1 (getActiveGpx) dead, Path 2 (incremental ADirectionInfo) significantly degraded UX, Path 3 (keep BRouter for routing) viable. User chose Option C — fork OsmAnd to add a route-export AIDL — so the epic is re-scoped around extending the AIDL surface rather than working around its limits.

Design doc at docs/option-c-aidl-design.md covers all five required sections (AIDL surface, parcelables, versioning, fork applicationId + manifest, build pipeline, upstream-PR plan) with concrete filenames/signatures that unblock glass-nav-nzt, glass-nav-ei1, glass-nav-45y, glass-nav-p9i, glass-nav-avi, glass-nav-c7v.

Gradle plumbing in OsmAnd sibling checkout: glassnav productFlavor + AndroidManifest-glassnav.xml. assembleGlassnavOpengldebugArmonlyDebug --dry-run BUILD SUCCESSFUL. On-device install + bind verification deferred to glass-nav-p9i since that's the task that exercises the bind path.

Added ANavigationProgress, ANavigationProgressParams, ARerouteEvent parcelables + AIDL methods registerForNavigationProgress/registerForRerouteEvents on IOsmAndAidlInterface + onNavigationProgress/onReroute on IOsmAndAidlCallback. Wired OsmandAidlServiceV2 dispatcher (KEY_ON_NAVIGATION_PROGRESS=64, KEY_ON_REROUTE_EVENT=128) and OsmandAidlApi listener bodies using IRoutingDataUpdateListener (1Hz, intervalMs-debounced) + IRouteInformationListener (bumps shared routeFingerprint consumed by glass-nav-nzt). osmand-api wrapper module builds clean; full OsmAnd app link blocked by unrelated pre-existing missing drawables (glass-nav-45y). On-device smoke test deferred to glass-nav-hva follow-up per acceptance criteria.

Implementation complete. ARoute, ARouteTurn, AGetRouteParams parcelables (.aidl + .java) match design doc §2. getActiveRoute() declared on IOsmAndAidlInterface (line 999), dispatched in OsmandAidlServiceV2 (line 1607), and implemented in OsmandAidlApi.getActiveRoute (line 2166): drains RoutingHelper.getCurrentCalculatedRoute() for polyline, iterates getRouteDirections() for turns, populates distanceFromStartM via wholeDistance - getDistanceFromPoint(offset), handles roundabout exitNumber, stamps shared routeFingerprint (which glass-nav-ei1's ARerouteEvent also bumps). osmand-api wrapper module assembles clean — new .aidl picked up automatically. On-device 'callable from stub test app returns populated parcelable' check deferred to glass-nav-vzm follow-up, which depends on glass-nav-p9i (the phone-side bind path).

Implemented Option C phone-side wiring. AndroidManifest: added net.osmand.glassnav to <queries>. OsmAndAidlClient.kt: prepended net.osmand.glassnav to KNOWN_PACKAGES (fork preferred when both installed); added navigationProgress(intervalMs): Flow<ANavigationProgress> (debounced subscribe/unsubscribe via ANavigationProgressParams.callbackId, matching existing navigationUpdates pattern); added rerouteEvents(): Flow<ARerouteEvent> (uses ANavigationUpdateParams per fork AIDL); added getActiveRoute(): Pair<ARoute, Long>? returning route+fingerprint; added hasOptionCExtensions() feature probe per design doc §3 (catches NoSuchMethodError + RemoteException so phone degrades gracefully on stock OsmAnd). NoopAidlCallback extended with onNavigationProgress/onReroute overrides. Debug activity (OsmAndAidlDebugActivity) gained 4 buttons: hasOptionCExtensions, getActiveRoute, subscribe nav progress, subscribe reroute. ./gradlew :phone-app:compileDebugKotlin and :phone-app:testDebugUnitTest both BUILD SUCCESSFUL. On-device bind+round-trip verification deferred to glass-nav-vzm (depends on this issue per design).

RideService.computeReroute and RoutePreviewFragment now go through computeOsmAndRoute (phone-app/src/main/java/dev/glass/phone/routing/OsmAndRouter.kt): subscribe to rerouteEvents, call navigate(), await first event, fetch ARoute via getActiveRoute(), map to (List<LatLng>, List<Turn>). BRouter + GpxTurnExtractor remain on disk but have no live call sites — deletion deferred to glass-nav-mcb. Phone-side RouteMatcher continues to drive off-route detection per the acceptance criteria.
Closed

RideService.pushRoute now calls OsmAndSnippetRenderer(applicationContext).render(route.turns) instead of SnippetRenderer.render. New OsmAndSnippetRenderer (phone-app/src/main/java/dev/glass/phone/osmand/OsmAndSnippetRenderer.kt) writes a ±50m GPX segment per turn to cacheDir/route-snippets/, hands OsmAnd a FileProvider URI with READ grant, calls OsmAndAidlClient.getBitmapForGpx (640x360, density 2.5, Color.RED — matches spike glass-nav-0ha), compresses to PNG, revokes the grant and deletes the tmp GPX. Added cache-path entry to res/xml/spike_file_paths.xml so the existing dev.glass.phone.spike.fileprovider authority covers the new cacheDir path. RideService imports for SnippetRenderer/MapDataSource/approachBearingDeg/OrientationPrefs/withContext removed; renderer field + ensureRenderer() helper + onDestroy renderer.close() removed. North-up only (getBitmapForGpx has no rotation param) — orientation toggle still triggers requestSnippetRefresh but the resulting snippets are identical content; cleanup deferred. If OsmAnd not installed or bind fails, all turn PNGs are empty bytes (existing TURN_BUNDLE behavior preserved). ./gradlew :phone-app:compileDebugKotlin and :testDebugUnitTest BUILD SUCCESSFUL. SnippetRenderer + MapDataSource still referenced by RoutePreviewFragment, RideControlsFragment, RouteDataPrefetcher, and the spike — deletion is glass-nav-mcb.

Deleted BRouter/Mapsforge dead code: BRouterClient.kt, BRouterTiles.kt, RouteDataPrefetcher.kt, MapsforgeRegionResolver.kt, DataDownloader.kt, SnippetRenderer.kt, MapDataSource.kt, MapTheme.kt, PositionArrowMarker.kt, RouteSnippetCache.kt, GpxTurnExtractor.kt, OrientationPrefs.kt, DataDownloadDialogFragment.kt, SpikeSnippetCapture.kt, berlin.map, btools.routingapp AIDL + corresponding *Test.kt files. Removed Mapsforge / androidsvg / slf4j / kxml2 / btools.routingapp deps from build.gradle.kts and manifest <queries>. RoutePreviewFragment + RideControlsFragment rewritten: dropped in-app Mapsforge MapView + RouteDataPrefetcher + storage-permission flow; both now show data only and offer an 'Open in OsmAnd' button that launches the OsmAnd app via getLaunchIntentForPackage (route is already live in OsmAnd via AIDL). RideViewModel.RouteState lost Downloading + NeedsStoragePermission; MainActivity branches dropped accordingly. RideService.requestSnippetRefresh removed (snippets are north-up only post glass-nav-y3q). NavigationMode no longer carries BRouter profile/vehicle fields. RoutingException moved to its own routing/RoutingException.kt. spike_file_paths.xml trimmed to just route-snippets cache path (OsmAndSnippetRenderer still uses it). SpikeSessionActivity: snippet capture button + function + snippetTurns + readme snippet section removed. ./gradlew :phone-app:assembleDebug + :testDebugUnitTest BUILD SUCCESSFUL.

Build succeeded; APK at OsmAnd/OsmAnd/build/outputs/apk/glassnavOpengldebugArmonly/debug/OsmAnd-glassnav-opengldebug-armonly-debug.apk (1.16 GB). Full recipe saved as memory osmand-glassnav-build-recipe. Unblocks glass-nav-vzm on-device verification.
Verified on-device: Glass snippet/instruction/TTS now track the current OsmAnd step, distance corresponds to the displayed instruction.
Verified on-device after parent bug closure.
On-device verification PASS. See notes for full empirical results.

Switched OsmandAidlApi.registerForNavigationProgress to per-id callback delivery (navProgressTargets map) instead of broadcasting to all KEY_ON_NAVIGATION_PROGRESS subscribers. Eliminates the duplicate-emit class caused by sibling subscribers / transient overlap. glassnav APK builds clean.

Closed
Closed
Closed

Render to 736×736 square with wider polyline window (300m vs 150m), rotate for TRAVEL_UP, crop central 640×360. Eliminates black corners at any rotation since destination diagonal (~734px) fits inside source. Applied to both orientations for visual consistency.

Composite build wired (includeBuild('OsmAnd') in root settings.gradle.kts). protocol module gets group=com.goodanser.osmglass version=1.0. OsmAnd/OsmAnd/build-common.gradle adds implementation 'com.goodanser.osmglass:protocol:1.0'. Empty GlassNavPlugin.java at OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/plugins/glassnav/, registered in PluginsHelper after AccessibilityPlugin+OsmandDevelopmentPlugin. ./gradlew help passes.

Ported transport (RfcommTransport, TransportFactory, TransportPrefs, GlassDeviceFinder) and render (OsmAndSnippetRenderer, SnippetBounds, TurnTypeMapping, Geo) packages under net.osmand.plus.plugins.glassnav.{transport,render}. TurnTypeMapping rewired to net.osmand.router.TurnType (constant values match AIDL 1:1). OsmAndSnippetRenderer's bitmap source stubbed pending Step 3. Compile validated: ./gradlew :OsmAnd:compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugKotlin succeeds. WIRING CHANGE: Step 1's composite-build approach didn't auto-substitute the protocol dep into the included build. Switched to subproject mapping — added include :glass-protocol with projectDir = file(../protocol) in OsmAnd/settings.gradle, and OsmAnd's build-common.gradle uses implementation project(:glass-protocol). Removed includeBuild(OsmAnd) from root settings.gradle.kts. BUILD GOTCHA: system locale is POSIX, run OsmAnd gradle commands with LC_ALL=C.utf8 to handle non-ASCII filenames.

Created GlassNavController.kt (singleton orchestrator: transport lifecycle, RouteStart/TurnBundle on connect, per-tick Progress, TurnAlert on segment-index transition, RouteEnd OFFROUTE on deviation, RouteEnd ARRIVED on final turn; CoroutineScope SupervisorJob+Dispatchers.IO; pairedMacProvider injected) and GlassNavRoutingListener.kt (IRouteInformationListener + IRoutingDataUpdateListener). GlassNavPlugin.init wires both, holds strong references (RoutingHelper uses WeakReference). disable() removes listeners + shuts down controller. Build: BUILD SUCCESSFUL on :OsmAnd:compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugKotlin with LC_ALL=C.utf8. CARRIED-OVER TODOs: TurnBundle.pngBytes is empty (renderBitmap still stubbed); Progress marker fields are MARKER_NONE; bearingDelta100 is 0; pairedMacProvider stub returns null until Step 5; MapOrientation hard-coded NORTH_UP until Step 5; no FGS until Step 4.

Added GlassStreamingService.kt (Service subclass, FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_CONNECTED_DEVICE on Q+, LOW-importance notification on channel 'glass_nav_stream', companion start/stop helpers). GlassNavController now starts/stops the service alongside transport (idempotent via AtomicBoolean). AndroidManifest-glassnav.xml gets the service declaration (exported=false, foregroundServiceType=connectedDevice) + FOREGROUND_SERVICE_CONNECTED_DEVICE permission. Other BLE/notification permissions already in shared AndroidManifest.xml. Build: BUILD SUCCESSFUL on :OsmAnd:compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugKotlin. CARRIED-OVER TODOs: notification icon uses android.R.drawable.stat_sys_data_bluetooth placeholder; pairedMacProvider still returns null pending Step 5.

Settings + pairing UI complete. GlassNavSettings.java facade over OsmandSettings (pairedMac, topSlot, bottomSlot, ttsMuted, mapOrientation — all .makeGlobal().makeShared()). GlassNavSettingsFragment.java extends BaseSettingsFragment, pattern from AntPlusSettingsFragment. GlassDevicePairingDialog.kt enumerates bonded BT devices, requests BLUETOOTH_CONNECT on 12+. SettingsScreenType.GLASS_NAV_SETTINGS added. GlassNavPlugin constructs settings, exposes getController()/getGlassNavSettings(), overrides getSettingsScreenType, reads name/desc from strings.xml. GlassNavController consumes settings (mapOrientation, slots, TTS) and exposes onSettingsChanged() for live mid-ride updates. 19 glass_nav_* strings added. Builds: BUILD SUCCESSFUL on both compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugKotlin (1m15s) and compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugJavaWithJavac (1m2s).

End-to-end on device confirmed working: pairing dialog opens (after routing tap through onPreferenceClick override), MAC persists into GlassNavSettings, RfcommTransport connects, RouteStart+TurnBundles arrive on Glass, Progress streams, map snippets render. Three bugs fixed mid-test: (1) BaseSettingsFragment.registerPreferences overwrote our pair-pref click listener — moved to onPreferenceClick override; (2) GlassNavSettings.pairedMac and legacy TransportPrefs.glass_mac were unsynced stores, so the controller's MAC check passed but GlassDeviceFinder fell back to name heuristic — fixed by passing explicit MAC into TransportFactory.create(ctx, mac); (3) RfcommTransport.cancelDiscovery() required runtime BLUETOOTH_SCAN on Android 12+ — dropped the call (we never start discovery). Renderer wired to in-process TrackBitmapDrawer (SharedUtil.loadGpxFile + MapBitmapDrawerListener async, 8s timeout) replacing the Step-2 stub.

Deleted phone-app/ and osmand-api/ from filesystem. Removed both from include(...) in root settings.gradle.kts. Builds verified: :glass-app:assembleDebug BUILD SUCCESSFUL (15s); OsmAnd :OsmAnd:compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugKotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL (46s). Only lingering reference is a doc comment string in protocol/src/test/.../PairEmulatorE2eTest.java mentioning 'phone-app role from' — not a code dependency. Option C AIDL extensions in the OsmAnd fork left in place per plan.

Epic complete. phone-app eliminated; all functionality now in OsmGlass (OsmAnd fork) as an in-process OsmandPlugin at OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/plugins/glassnav/. Auto-streams to paired Glass on RoutingHelper.newRouteIsCalculated. Settings live under Menu→Plugins→Glass Nav. All 7 sub-beads closed. End-to-end smoke test passed on real device with Glass headset.
